items.title: Whooping cough cases more than double in southern WA

children.content: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds up a needle next to a vial.

items.title: 'Not about race': Winmar statue removed two days after assault conviction

image.altText: A crane removes the statue of Nicky Winmar.

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

items.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry — including Noongar game developer Kat Gledhill-Tucker.

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia’s bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

image.altText: Black swans at swan river in Perth.

items.title: Rare barking geckos and Bungle Bungle sliders face extinction

children.content: New research has revealed the Pilbara barking gecko and Bungle Bungle robust slider are facing a heightened risk of extinction due to a rapidly warming climate.

image.altText: Close up shot of Pilbara barking gecko on a red stone

articles.title: Meet Mr Row Row, the man who invented Norway's viral World Cup Viking row

children.content: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

children.content: Two goals from Erling Haaland deliver Norway an upset win over Brazil in New Jersey, before Mexico hosts England at the Azteca. Follow live.

items.title: The world is getting pure Trump and the US presidency may never be the same

children.content: Donald Trump is undertaking regime change, not just in Venezuela, but on US soil and it is unlikely the presidency will ever be the same.

children.content: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

items.title: What household debt levels tell us about the rise of One Nation

children.content: The paradox of a strong economy and stock market with very low consumer sentiment is not close to being resolved. Quite the opposite, the disconnect is, if anything, widening.

items.title: The 'dirty little secret' in active funds management and ETFs boom

children.content: A record number of exchange traded funds were added to the Australian share market in the past financial year.

items.title: ASX set to slip despite strength in global markets

children.content: The ASX looks likely to open lower despite the Gulf cease fire holding and a positive sentiment on global markets. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Is a full day of screen time for kids ever OK?

children.content: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

items.title: From kitchen to screen: WA band's song features in new Jackass movie

children.content: Geraldton band Ratsalad thought they were being scammed when the producer of the latest Jackass movie said they wanted to feature their song in the credits.

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: 'Truly special': Australia wins T20 World Cup by downing England in final

children.content: Australia has won the ICC Women's T20 World Cup in commanding style, defeating England by seven wickets in the final at Lord's.

items.title: Experts and union warn about ABC's AI use

children.content: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

items.title: Inside the life of the Australian man accused of Thai girl's murder

children.content: Accused murderer Simon Peter Carman spent much of the past decade isolating himself from many of his friends and family in Australia. It's in his rented room in Thailand that local police allege a gruesome murder occurred.

articles.title: Killed teen's father rejects idea of leniency payment from Australian

children.content: While the public will remember Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla for the tragic nature of her death, her aunt, Mee Boonsert, will remember the baby she helped raise.

items.title: AFP to push Australia's policing model to UN

children.content: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

items.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?

items.title: The hidden brain disease impacting DV victim-survivors

children.content: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

items.title: 'We can't wait': WA town hopeful of economic benefits of reopening gold mine

children.content: The rebirth of the mothballed Badgebup Gold Mine is expected to bring new industry and inject hundreds of workers into one of WA's premier farming regions.
